name: Electricity Maps API
what_it_does: Electricity Maps API provides real-time and historical carbon intensity of electricity (gCO2eq/kWh) and renewable energy percentage by country and region worldwide, enabling sustainability-aware applications.
use_cases: Green computing: shifting compute workloads to low-carbon grid hours/regions
Carbon accounting for cloud and data center emissions
Sustainability dashboards showing real-time grid carbon intensity
EV charging optimization based on grid cleanliness
Corporate carbon footprint reporting and Scope 2 emissions tracking
not_for: Raw electricity pricing or market data (not primary focus)
Grid frequency or power quality monitoring
Free commercial use (non-commercial free tier only)
best_when: An agent needs real-time or historical carbon intensity data globally to make sustainability decisions — compute scheduling, carbon accounting, or green infrastructure optimization.
avoid_when: You need raw energy pricing data, need commercial use for free, or require granularity below 5-minute intervals.

auth.notes: API key passed via auth-token header. Free tier available for non-commercial use with application. Commercial use requires paid plan. Keys scoped by zone access (some zones paywalled).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Non-commercial use: limited zone access, current data only (no history)
pricing.paid_tiers: Commercial plans from ~$200/month; enterprise pricing for full historical data and all zones
pricing.notes: Free tier requires application and approval for non-commercial use. Paid tiers unlock historical data, all zones, and SLAs. Pricing not fully public — contact sales for commercial.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; free tier is restrictive

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec published; JSON error responses with clear messages. Zone access errors (403) clearly indicate plan limitations. Documentation is well-structured with zone codes and data methodology explained.
known_agent_gotchas: Not all zones have real-time data — some zones use modeled/estimated values; check data source field
Zone codes are country/region codes (DE, FR, US-CAL-CISO) not always intuitive — consult zone list first
Free tier blocks access to historical data entirely — agents needing trends must use paid tier
Carbon intensity values can be null when data is unavailable; agents must handle null gracefully
Data has 5–15 minute delay from real-time; not suitable for millisecond-level decisions
Some zones aggregate multiple grid regions — granularity varies by country

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key in header (better than query param). No fine-grained scopes but zone-level access control provides some segmentation. GDPR-compliant as EU-based company. Open source data pipeline allows methodology inspection.
